extraction.one_line_summary: KING Benjamin attacked on uncategorized pre-climb ramps hitting 12%, survived brutal solo effort to win by 48 seconds over MØRKØV.
tactical_patterns: KING Benjamin launched decisive move on uncategorized 9-12% pre-climb sections before official climb, exposing poor pacing by chasers.
DE GENDT Thomas waved MATÉ through mountain points sprint, appearing to protest perceived bullying of polka dot jersey.
GC group delayed response until final sections allowed KING's gap to become irretrievable despite MØRKØV's chase closing to 48 seconds.

rider_signals.KING Benjamin: positive: demonstrated exceptional ability to sustain power through variable gradient spikes (9-12%) when launched before categorized climb begins
rider_signals.MØRKØV Michael: positive: closed significant gap to 48 seconds showing unexpected climbing durability for sprinter-domestique in breakaway scenario
rider_signals.LÓPEZ Miguel Ángel: positive: finished strongest of GC favorites in reduced group, suggesting form building compared to earlier Vuelta weakness
rider_signals.KELDERMAN Wilco: positive: pushed pace hardest through finale among GC group, more aggressive than expected for climbing domestique role
rider_signals.YATES Simon: neutral: retained red jersey but allowed breakaway excessive gap, conservative tactics when holding race lead
rider_signals.IZAGIRRE Gorka: positive: contradicted own stated weakness claims by making GC selection, sandbagging before mountain stage
rider_signals.DE GENDT Thomas: neutral: political gesture waving MATÉ through points suggests team dynamics affecting breakaway cooperation
